title: The Macro Minute: Will reopening the US gov’t spur a Santa Claus rally?

skipper here at Darius Del to present
our macro minute for Monday November
10th 2025. Hope everyone had a great
weekend. So as always we'll start with
the executive summary from today's
leadoff morning note. So let's hop right
in. Today's key macro question is will
reopening the US government spl
isolation because it does not adequately
mitigate the key risk to asset markets
i.e. another policy mistake by the tardy
pow fed. Terms of the analytical nuance,
markets are back to pricing in the
trending risk on market regime condition
to start the week as optimism grew that
US lawmakers were nearing a deal to end
the government shutdown after eight
Senate Democrats cross party lines to
help Republicans advance a funding bill
on the 40th day of the impass. With a
record-breaking shutdown nearing its
conclusion, Democrats fell short of
securing renewed tax credits to curb
health insurance premium hikes and
failed to prove their ability to
negotiate effectively in the second
Trump administration. Despite their
legislative setback, Democrats found
soloulless inent polling that showed
voters generally blamed Republicans more
for the deadlock. While the Trump
administration faced backlash for urging
states to cut food aid for 42 million
low-income Americans, the central issue
extending Obamacare tax credits remains
widely popular as 24 million Americans
face premium hikes costing hundreds more
per month for health insurance.
Democrats plan to use the forthcoming
healthcare tax credit vote as a midterm
campaign springboard, framing
Republicans as responsible for soaring
insurance premiums if they block the
legislation or the extension rather. Uh,
ending the shutdown will restore
visibility into critical economic data
like the two missing jobs reports,
September PC and October CPI, which is
due this Thursday, clarifying the
near-term outlook for monetary policy.
Investors should note that resumed data
releases may expose compromised data
quality after Bureau of Labor Statistics
halted in-person collection. While
making sense of the upcoming macro data
will prove challenging and yield less
than desirable signals, the micro data
continues to strongly support our
bullish paradigm CE. As always, members
of our global investor community can
trust that our institutional grade risk
management overlays kiss and Dr. remote
help our portfolios navigate these
emergent risks better than investors
attempting to manage risk without
systematic rules or relying on
fundamental research alone.
Transitioning to our 42 micro dashboard
as always we'll wrap up with a question
from our community. This one's titled
February recap question says, "Hi
Darius, I'm a newer subscriber. I
understand your process guide you out of
risk assets before the April draw down."
That is correct. I've never heard you
explain what that looked like uh having
not been a subscriber at the time. Uh
could maybe run through a recap of what
that looked like uh in your models. Uh
thanks. So actually we have a chart here
that sort of explains the progression of
uh signals within KISS uh and Dr. Mo um
you know in and around the 2025 crash.
And so uh the first signal to reduce
exposure uh in half came on March 5th.
Uh and so that was after a 6% draw down
uh in the stock market. Uh and then the
second uh exposure to take the position
to 0%. So cut in half on the orange
line, the red line here on March 6 took
the exposure to 0%. There was a further
13% decline to the ultimate trough. Is
that more than 13% decline intraday? I
think it was like 17% intraday. Uh but
there was a 13% clo close to close
decline from the time uh Kiss and Dr. Mo
got us to 0% stock market exposure. Uh
and then on April 30th, uh KISS and Dr.
Mo took up S&P 500 exposure uh to half
position. And then here on uh June 2nd,
uh when we um it took up the S&P
exposure back up to 100%. And so this is
the whole point of what we're trying to
get accomplished here uh in the context
of our risk management frameworks is
avoid this left tail the distribution
between the red dotted red line and the
first uh light green line. If you can
take that out of your return
distribution you do you accomplish a
couple things. One uh you eliminate the
symptom variance in your portfolio
returns and so ultimately you wind up
with higher risk adjusted returns. And
for those of you maybe uh unfamiliar,
semivariance is the down is the it's
it's essentially the standard deviation
but only focusing on the the the
downside samples. So the the negative
price returns and so if you eliminate if
you reduce the amount of semivariance in
your portfolio then obviously all the
risk adjusted return metrics uh improve.
So that's great for any institutional
investor. But from the setup of a retail
investor whose sole responsibility for
or sole goal of investing or at least
your goal of investing should be to
retire on time and comfortably. What
eliminating the market return between
the dark red line and the and the light
green line does for you uh in terms of
achieving your uh goal to retire on time
and comfortably is it it capture a
portfolio at a higher net asset value so
that when you start investing again in
the subsequent bull market and again
this one started within a matter of you
know what's called six weeks the next
one might take six months the next one
might take a couple of years as we've
seen in the post.com bubble and
obviously in the GFC you don't know when
the next bull market's going to start So
what you're trying to do in that
situation is stop your net asset value
from declining at a at an elevated level
i.e. book gains from the prep prior bull
market so that when the new bull market
starts if it's six weeks, six months,
six years, who knows how long it'll take
in the future. uh when the new bull
market starts, you start investing from
a higher net asset value than all the
people who had to go through this uh all
these negative returns uh and stress uh
and and all the other negative
consequences both physically and
financially that come alongside uh being
long in a bare market uh you know you
can avoid all that stuff from your
portfolio. So again, higher risk
adjusted return for institutional
investors. Uh um compounding your
portfolio from a higher net asset value
in the subsequent bull market is the
benefit for uh retail investors. And
obviously both of those will help you
retire on time and comfortably if you're
a retail investor or you know stay in
your seat as a buy side professional and
achieve the bonus uh goals that you may
have uh as an investment professional.
So we'll wrap it up there. Derry St here
